preview_text:
The model favors Argentina at 74%, with Algeria assigned only a 5% chance of victory. The underdog faces a steep deficit in the statistical forecast, though the margin leaves room for upset outcomes.

The 21% draw probability suggests moderate competitive balance despite Argentina's clear advantage. The win-loss split widens considerably in Argentina's favor, indicating the model expects the match to lean decisively toward one outcome rather than remain tightly contested throughout. A draw remains plausible but the modal scenario is an Argentine victory.

The model and market diverge notably on Algeria's prospects. The market consensus—both sportsbooks and Polymarket—assigns the underdog roughly double the model's win probability, centering on 10–11% rather than 5%. The model and market align closely on Argentina (74% vs. 69%) and draw (21% vs. 20%). This gap on Algeria's tail probability is the main point of disagreement; the dashboard does not claim to know which assessment is correct.
